issues.body: The test documentation says the captured manifest and AGE_OPERAND_ENC must stay consistent, but `if let Some(map_hex)` makes the check optional. If AGE_OPERAND_ENC[CAPTURED_AGE] is deleted or its key is changed, this test remains green even though the captured and live-proving configurations no longer share the asserted anchor entry. That is a vacuous pass on one of the test's stated load-bearing invariants.
issues.fix_hint: Require the captured-age entry with `.find(...).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...))` (or an equivalent assertion), then compare it to OPERAND_ENC unconditionally. Add or structure the test so deleting the CAPTURED_AGE entry demonstrably fails.
issues.title: Captured-age map consistency check silently passes when the entry is missing

verdict.summary: The native-encoding comparisons are useful and exercise the committed site files, but the claimed cross-file invariant is not actually enforced: removing the captured age from AGE_OPERAND_ENC makes the consistency test silently skip its assertion. The guard should fail when that entry is absent.
